evidence_text: I'm sorry, but I was unable to find any relevant information in memory. Here are the factual bullets drawn directly from the source:
As of mid-2025, there are numerous AI code agent tools available.
The author initially had extreme skepticism towards AI agents and their impact on software engineering.
Prior to February 2025, AI exposure was limited to GitHub Copilot's single-line auto-complete, which was considered a toy due to poor context collection.
The author has recently begun exploring targeted agent use in production codebases but remained cautiously skeptical due to past experiences cleaning up AI-produced code.
The following four AI coding tools were selected for testing: Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI, and Gemini.
Claude Code is a CLI offering from Anthropic, supporting code diffing in IDEs, MCP tools, and Anthropic's LLMs.
Cursor is a fully integrated IDE experience, using Claude Sonnet 4 for all tests, with a range of model choices but sticking to Sonnet 4 for consistency.
Gemini CLI is mentioned as a tool.
Codex is mentioned as having a powerful model hampered by UX issues.
Cursor is noted to lead in setup speed, Docker/Render deployment, and code quality.
Claude Code is best for rapid prototypes and a productive terminal UX.
Gemini CLI wins for large-context refactors.
